top_stories.title: Phishing and MFA exploitation: Targeting the keys to the kingdom
top_stories.summary: A 2025 security analysis identifies growing attacker focus on exploiting weaknesses in MFA workflows and using compromised credentials from trusted accounts to conduct phishing campaigns. The report highlights how attackers are systematically undermining authentication trust mechanisms rather than bypassing them outright.

top_stories.title: Cross‑tenant helpdesk impersonation to data exfiltration: A human-operated intrusion playbook
top_stories.summary: Threat actors are conducting social engineering attacks via Microsoft Teams by impersonating IT helpdesk staff to trick users into granting remote access across tenant boundaries. The attack chain leverages legitimate administrative tools and standard protocols for lateral movement and data exfiltration, exploiting cross-tenant collaboration features in Microsoft's platform.

top_stories.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
top_stories.summary: A Vercel employee's access to an AI tool resulted in a data breach involving stolen OAuth tokens. The incident highlights OAuth tokens as an emerging attack vector for lateral movement in enterprise environments.

top_stories.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
top_stories.summary: The Bitwarden CLI npm package was compromised by attackers who uploaded a malicious @bitwarden/cli package containing a credential-stealing payload. The attack targeted developer credentials and had the potential to spread to other projects through the npm dependency chain.

top_stories.title: Google brings instant email verification to Android, no OTP needed
top_stories.summary: Google has introduced cryptographically verified email credentials for Android via the Credential Manager API, enabling authentication without OTP codes. The API aligns with the W3C Digital Credential API standard and provides a unified method for apps to request and retrieve user credentials for authentication and authorization.

news.title: Regular Password Resets Aren’t as Safe as You Think
news.summary: Specops Software has published research showing that regular password resets can be exploited by attackers through helpdesk social engineering to achieve full account compromise. The analysis highlights how seemingly legitimate reset requests can be used to bypass authentication controls at the helpdesk level.

news.title: CISA orders feds to patch BlueHammer flaw exploited as zero-day
news.summary: CISA has issued a directive ordering U.S. federal agencies to patch a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation vulnerability known as BlueHammer, which has been actively exploited as a zero-day. The flaw involves privilege escalation, making it directly relevant to access control and endpoint security within federal IAM environments.

news.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Helpdesk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
news.summary: A threat group called UNC6692 is using Microsoft Teams impersonation of IT helpdesk staff to deploy custom malware called SNOW on victim systems. The attack relies on social engineering to convince targets to accept Teams chat invitations from accounts posing as internal IT support, a technique increasingly common in enterprise intrusions.

news.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
news.summary: The Bitwarden CLI package (@bitwarden/cli@2026.4.0) was compromised as part of an ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, with malicious code published in a file called 'bw1.js' within the package. Bitwarden is a widely used password manager and credential management tool relied upon by IAM and security professionals for secrets and identity management workflows.

news.title: Vercel Finds More Compromised Accounts in Context.ai-Linked Breach
news.summary: Vercel disclosed additional compromised customer accounts discovered during an expanded investigation into a security breach linked to Context.ai that allowed unauthorized access to its internal systems. The investigation was expanded to include additional compromise indicators and a review of requests to the Vercel network and environment.

news.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
news.summary: Researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network for AI agents, exposed 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents via an unsecured database. The breach also revealed plaintext third-party credentials including OpenAI API keys within private messages, raising concerns about cross-application permission stacking and credential exposure in AI agent ecosystems.

news.title: Consumer Identity and Access Management Global Market Forecast Report 2026-2036, Profiles of Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, Amazon Web Services, Accenture
news.summary: A market forecast report projects the global Consumer Identity and Access Management (CIAM) market will grow from USD 12.6 billion in 2026 to USD 40.2 billion by 2036, representing a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers key vendors including Okta, Auth0, Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Microsoft, Salesforce, IBM, AWS, and Accenture.

news.title: Non-Human Identity Access Management Market Global Forecast Report 2026-2036: Opportunities in Adoption of Zero Trust Architecture, Cloud-native Applications, and Regulatory Compliance Requirements
news.summary: A new market forecast report projects the global non-human identity (NHI) access management market will grow from USD 12.2 billion in 2026 to USD 38.8 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 12.2%. The report covers market dynamics across regions and sectors, with key growth drivers including zero trust architecture adoption, cloud-native applications, and regulatory compliance requirements.

news.title: No Exploit Needed: How Attackers Walk Through the Front Door via Identity-Based Attacks
news.summary: The article examines identity-based attacks as the dominant initial access vector in breaches, focusing on stolen credentials obtained through methods such as credential stuffing. It argues that despite industry focus on sophisticated threats, compromised credentials remain the most reliable entry point for attackers.

news.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
news.summary: CISA added eight vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including CVE-2023-27351, an improper authentication flaw in PaperCut. The remaining vulnerabilities primarily affect Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager and other non-IAM systems, with federal remediation deadlines set for April-May 2026.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. His admitted crimes involved SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies, including notable breaches attributed to Scattered Spider such as those involving Okta and MGM Resorts.

news.title: Vercel Breach Tied to Context AI Hack Exposes Limited Customer Credentials
news.summary: Vercel disclosed a security breach originating from a compromise of third-party AI tool Context.ai, which was used by a Vercel employee. The attacker leveraged that access to take over the employee's Google Workspace account and gain unauthorized access to certain internal Vercel systems, exposing limited customer credentials.

news.title: Making opportunistic cyberattacks harder by design
news.summary: Microsoft published details on how it secures Dynamics 365 and Power Platform through credential removal and attack surface reduction. The approach uses platform engineering techniques to block opportunistic threats, with credential elimination being a central IAM-relevant component.

news.title: Securely connecting on-premises data systems to Amazon Redshift with IAM Roles Anywhere
news.summary: AWS published a guide explaining how to use IAM Roles Anywhere with Amazon Redshift to establish secure, private connections for on-premises data systems. The solution eliminates the need for long-lived access keys by leveraging certificate-based authentication to obtain temporary IAM credentials.

news.title: AWS Managed Microsoft AD is now available on Windows functional level 2016
news.summary: AWS has upgraded all AWS Managed Microsoft AD directories to Windows functional level 2016, applied automatically to existing directories. The upgrade to AWS Directory Service for Microsoft AD brings access to newer Active Directory features associated with the 2016 functional level.

news.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
news.summary: Threat actors behind the Tycoon 2FA phishing-as-a-service platform have shifted tactics to adopt device code phishing attacks. The technique exploits legitimate OAuth device authorization flows to bypass MFA, tricking victims into surrendering account access through a service's standard new-device login process.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free From the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to physical world security scenarios. It focuses on how 2FA can serve as an additional barrier against threat actors operating outside conventional IT infrastructure.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
